div.span: i built a dev dashboard because i didn't like my other dev dashboards
happiest when there's solder in the air
i speak microcontroller and server fluently
my media server notifies me via webhook. i built the webhook handler.
building an open-source robot arm. currently in pieces.
that feeling when the e-ink refreshes exactly right
i built a stream deck plugin that deploys my own SaaS
embedded + full-stack + whatever the project needs
yes, i control my 3d printer through a custom AI assistant
started simple. added docker. you know how it goes.
one button on my stream deck deploys crewvolution to prod
i write docs because future-me is grateful
i think about edge cases in the shower
i have more projects than hours in the day. working on both.
i spent hours on OBS overlays i never go live with
it was supposed to be a weekend project
built crewvolution so gaming events could stop using excel
ADA manages my printer, spotify, and smart home. she's fine.
wanted to check departures. ended up writing a captive portal.
scope creep is a feature
sonarr told me a file arrived. i automated what happened next.
the readme is longer than the actual project
built in NRW, shipped to the world
added one more feature. and then one more. and then one more.
my commit times say a lot about my sleep schedule
i automate things i could just do manually. it's a gift.
yes, those are different overlays. yes, i made them myself.
git blame says i did that. git blame is correct.
got tired of spreadsheets at gaming fairs
C++ in the morning. TypeScript at night.
i don't stop because i'm done. i stop because i'm tired.
documentation: written, occasionally read
i told myself i'd keep it simple this time
i like projects with sharp edges
building the tools i wish existed
